message.content:
You are working in the monorepo at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai.
  Your working directory for most edits is packages/web.

  ## Task: FL-02 — Built-in family catalog

  ### Branch
  git checkout main && git pull && git checkout -b fl-02-family-catalog

  ### Context

  FL-01 is already merged to main. The data-model scaffolding exists:

  packages/core/src/index.ts defines:
    - FamilyDiscipline = 'door' | 'window' | 'stair' | 'railing' | 'wall_type' | 'floor_type' | 'roof_type' | 'column' | 'beam' | 'generic'
    - family_type element: { kind, id, name, familyId, discipline, parameters, isBuiltIn? }
    - door element has: familyTypeId?, overrideParams?
    - window element has: familyTypeId?, overrideParams?
    - stair / railing have: overrideParams?

  packages/web/src/families/types.ts defines:
    - FamilyParamDef: { key, label, type: 'length_mm'|'angle_deg'|'material_key'|'boolean'|'option', default, options?, min?, max?, instanceOverridable }
    - FamilyDefinition: { id, name, discipline, thumbnail?, params, defaultTypes: { name, familyId, discipline, parameters, isBuiltIn: true }[] }
    - resolveParam(key, instanceOverrides, typeParameters, familyDef, inlineFallback): unknown

  The existing door geometry lives in packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx at function makeDoorMesh() (~line 799).
  It builds: head member, two jambs, panel leaf, threshold — all THREE.BoxGeometry inside a THREE.Group.
  Key values it currently uses: door.widthMm, wall.heightMm, wall.thicknessMm.

  The existing window geometry lives in the same file at function makeWindowMesh() (~line 853).
  It builds: head, sill, jambL, jambR, glazing pane, optional mullion — all THREE.BoxGeometry inside a THREE.Group.
  Key values: win.widthMm, win.heightMm, win.sillHeightMm, wall.thicknessMm.

  ### What to build

  Create these NEW files (do NOT touch Viewport.tsx yet — that is FL-03):

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE 1: packages/web/src/families/geometryFns/doorGeometry.ts
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Export a single function:

    import type * as THREE_NS from 'three';
    import type { Element } from '@bim-ai/core';
    import type { FamilyDefinition } from '../types';

    export type DoorGeomInput = {
      door:      Extract<Element, { kind: 'door' }>;
      wall:      Extract<Element, { kind: 'wall' }>;
      elevM:     number;
      paint:     ViewportPaintBundle | null;   // import from '../../viewport/materials'
      familyDef: FamilyDefinition | undefined;
    };

    export function buildDoorGeometry(input: DoorGeomInput): THREE_NS.Group

  The function is a clean port of the existing makeDoorMesh logic, but reads dimensions
  through resolveParam() so family/type/instance params override the inline fallbacks.
  Parameters to resolve:
    - 'leafWidthMm'  → fallback: door.widthMm clamped [350, 4000]
    - 'leafHeightMm' → fallback: wall.heightMm * 0.86 clamped [600, 2500]
    - 'frameDepthMm' → fallback: wall.thicknessMm clamped [80, 500]
    - 'frameSectMm'  → fallback: 70
    - 'panelThickMm' → fallback: 45

  The function must set group.userData.bimPickId = door.id on the returned group
  and on every child mesh. Use the same addEdges helper pattern (import or accept
  as a parameter — keep it simple, inline a thin addEdges if needed).

  Material creation: accept a `makeMat(color: string, roughness: number, metalness: number)` factory
  approach, or inline new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial — your choice, just keep it readable.

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE 2: packages/web/src/families/geometryFns/windowGeometry.ts
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Export a single function:

    export function buildWindowGeometry(input: WindowGeomInput): THREE_NS.Group

  Same pattern. Parameters to resolve:
    - 'widthMm'      → fallback: win.widthMm clamped [140, 4000]
    - 'heightMm'     → fallback: win.heightMm clamped [50, wall.heightMm - sillMm - 60]
    - 'sillMm'       → fallback: win.sillHeightMm clamped [60, wall.heightMm - 80]
    - 'frameDepthMm' → fallback: wall.thicknessMm + 20 clamped [60, 500]
    - 'frameSectMm'  → fallback: 60
    - 'glazingAlpha' → fallback: 0.35

  Port the existing makeWindowMesh geometry exactly — head, sill, two jambs,
  glazing pane (DoubleSide, transparent), optional center mullion when width > 1.2 m.
  Glazing color reads CSS token '--cat-glazing' with fallback '#c8d8ea'.

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE 3: packages/web/src/families/familyCatalog.ts
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Export:
    export const BUILT_IN_FAMILIES: FamilyDefinition[]
    export function getFamilyById(id: string): FamilyDefinition | undefined
    export function getTypeById(id: string): FamilyDefinition['defaultTypes'][number] | undefined

  Include exactly these door families (discipline: 'door'):

    Family "builtin:door:single" — "Single Leaf Door"
      params: leafWidthMm (length_mm, default 900, min 600, max 2400, instanceOverridable: true)
              leafHeightMm (length_mm, default 2100, min 1800, max 3000, instanceOverridable: false)
              frameSectMm (length_mm, default 70, instanceOverridable: false)
      defaultTypes:
        - id "builtin:door:single:900x2100"  name "Single 900×2100"  leafWidthMm: 900,  leafHeightMm: 2100
        - id "builtin:door:single:1000x2100" name "Single 1000×2100" leafWidthMm: 1000, leafHeightMm: 2100
        - id "builtin:door:single:800x2100"  name "Single 800×2100"  leafWidthMm: 800,  leafHeightMm: 2100

    Family "builtin:door:double" — "Double Leaf Door"
              leafHeightMm (length_mm, default 2100, min 1800, max 3000, instanceOverridable: false)
      defaultTypes:
        - id "builtin:door:double:1800x2100" name "Double 1800×2100" leafWidthMm: 1800, leafHeightMm: 2100
        - id "builtin:door:double:2100x2100" name "Double 2100×2100" leafWidthMm: 2100, leafHeightMm: 2100

  And these window families (discipline: 'window'):

    Family "builtin:window:casement" — "Casement Window"
      params: widthMm (length_mm, default 1200, min 400, max 3000, instanceOverridable: true)
              heightMm (length_mm, default 1500, min 400, max 2400, instanceOverridable: true)
              sillMm (length_mm, default 900, min 200, max 2000, instanceOverridable: true)
              glazingAlpha (boolean mapped to 0.35/0.6, default 0.35, instanceOverridable: false)
      defaultTypes:
        - id "builtin:window:casement:1200x1500" name "Casement 1200×1500" widthMm: 1200, heightMm: 1500, sillMm: 900
        - id "builtin:window:casement:600x1200"  name "Casement 600×1200"  widthMm: 600,  heightMm: 1200, sillMm: 900
        - id "builtin:window:casement:2400x1500" name "Casement 2400×1500" widthMm: 2400, heightMm: 1500, sillMm: 900

    Family "builtin:window:fixed" — "Fixed Glazing"
      params: widthMm (length_mm, default 1500, min 400, max 6000, instanceOverridable: true)
              heightMm (length_mm, default 2000, min 400, max 4000, instanceOverridable: true)
              sillMm (length_mm, default 100, min 0, max 1000, instanceOverridable: true)
      defaultTypes:
        - id "builtin:window:fixed:1500x2000" name "Fixed 1500×2000" widthMm: 1500, heightMm: 2000, sillMm: 100
        - id "builtin:window:fixed:3000x2400" name "Fixed 3000×2400" widthMm: 3000, heightMm: 2400, sillMm: 100

  ### Type imports / paths

    import { resolveParam, type FamilyDefinition, type FamilyParamDef } from './types'
    import type { Element } from '@bim-ai/core'
    import * as THREE from 'three'
    Import ViewportPaintBundle from '../../viewport/materials' (check the actual export name — it may be ViewportPaintBundle or similar)

  ### Acceptance criteria

  1. `pnpm -r typecheck` passes with no new errors.
  2. `pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test --run` passes.
  3. Each geometry file is pure — no React, no hooks, no store access.
  4. familyCatalog.ts has no Three.js import (it is data-only).
  5. resolveParam is used in both geometry fns; no direct `door.widthMm` etc. without going through it.

  ### After completing

  Run:
    pnpm -r typecheck
    pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test --run

  Then commit:
    git add packages/web/src/families/
    git commit -m "feat(families): FL-02 — built-in door + window family catalog + geometry fns"
    git push -u origin fl-02-family-catalog

  Do NOT merge to main. The orchestrator will merge after review.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
FL-02 is done. Here's what was created:

- **`families/types.ts`** — added `id: string` to `defaultTypes` items (needed for `getTypeById` and type-lookup in geometry fns)
- **`families/familyCatalog.ts`** — data-only, no Three.js; exports `BUILT_IN_FAMILIES`, `getFamilyById`, `getTypeById` with 4 families (2 door, 2 window) and 10 default types total
- **`families/geometryFns/doorGeometry.ts`** — pure geometry function; resolves all 5 params through `resolveParam()` (leafWidthMm, leafHeightMm, frameDepthMm, frameSectMm, panelThickMm), no direct `door.widthMm` access, sets `bimPickId` on group and every child mesh
- **`families/geometryFns/windowGeometry.ts`** — same pattern; resolves 6 params, ports head/sill/jambs/glazing/mullion geometry exactly, reads `--cat-glazing` CSS token with fallback

Both `pnpm -r typecheck` (0 errors) and `pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test --run` (1178/1178 tests passing) are clean.
